#!/bin/bash

set -e

case "$1" in
    configure)
        mkdir -p /etc/bebznet
        mkdir -p /opt/bebznet/data
        mkdir -p /usr/share/bebznet/templates

        chmod +x /usr/local/bin/bebznet-config

        chown -R root:root /etc/bebznet /opt/bebznet /usr/share/bebznet

        touch /etc/bebznet/.env-template

        cat << 'EOF'

╔════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║         apt-bebznet-suite installé !           ║
╚════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

Pour commencer, execute:
  $ sudo bebznet-config

Cette commande ouvre le menu de configuration

Services inclus :
  • Pi-hole + Unbound (DNS/AdBlocking)
  • Nginx (Reverse Proxy)
  • WireGuard Easy (VPN)
  • Uptime Kuma (Monitoring)
  • Portainer (Docker Management)
  • CrowdSec (Security)
  • Samba (File Sharing)
  • FileBrowser (Web File Manager)

EOF
        ;;
esac

#DEBHELPER#
